summary: Excellent soundboard recording of a uniquely set‑listed show with standout Terrapin Station, Drums/Space and the rare Eyes of the World/Fire on the Mountain combo.
review: The digital soundboard source is praised for crystal‑clear, fully present audio. Musically the concert shines in its second set – a phenomenal Terrapin Station into an expansive Drums/Space, plus the one‑time‑only pairing of Eyes of the World, Fire on the Mountain and Corrina – though a few listeners found parts of the first half uneven.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars highlighting strong performance and excellent sound; only one very negative review drags the average down, yielding an overall high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers repeatedly note the audio is crisp, fully present and free of drop‑outs or balance problems.

poor_songs: Terrapin Station (as noted by one reviewer)
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set opens bright with Here Comes Sunshine, Althea and a Bird Song → Promised Land pairing. The second set is the highlight: a long Terrapin Station into an expansive Drums/Space, followed by Eyes of the World → Fire on the Mountain, Corrina and Days Between – a sequence that only this 1994 show ever delivered.

band_member_comments.Jerry: One reviewer felt Garcia was “kicking his butt” making the music hard to listen to, while others simply called the show ‘kick‑ass’ without specific critique.
band_member_comments.Drums: Terrapin into Drums/Space described as phenomenal by most, though one fan found the extended drums/space segment overly long.
